text: The Correlator Backend System shall receive data from the Correlator in real-time.
The Correlator Backend System shall assemble time-series from the Correlator lag output.
The Correlator Backend System shall perform Fourier Transforms of the assembled time series.
The Correlator Backend System shall deliver formatted results to the End-to-End System.
The Correlator Backend System shall perform basic data assembly formatting and processing services.
The Correlator Backend System shall support real-time inspection of the astronomical data stream.
The Backend System shall replace all invalid data with zero values.
The Backend System shall keep track of data invalid counts.
The Backend System shall notify M&C of any detected interruptions of data delivery from the Correlator.
The Backend System shall verify successful delivery of output to the e2e.
The Backend System shall acknowledge receipt of all data received from M&C.
The Backend System shall guarantee safe delivery of all internal messages.
The Backend System shall be able to handle lag sets up to a maximum size of 262144 values.
The Backend System shall store input data records in a memory buffer.
The Backend System shall update internal parameter tables to synchronize with new operational conditions.
The Backend System shall apply normalizations based on reported data invalid counts.
The Backend System shall apply coarse quantization corrections based on state count or quantizer power measurement data.
The Backend System shall perform windowing operations prior and subsequent to Fourier Transform.
The Backend System shall perform power-of-two complex-to-complex Fast Fourier Transforms.
The Backend System shall sum the frequency domain spectral results.
The Backend System shall store formatted output data records in a memory buffer with backup disk buffering.
The Backend System shall monitor data transfer rates from the Correlator and to the e2e.
The Backend System shall monitor the overall data processing rate.
The Backend System shall trap flag and repair computation errors including infinities and NaNs.
The Backend System shall check Backend physical processors to ensure they are responding.
The Backend System shall provide the ability to initiate a reboot of any Backend physical processor.
The Backend System shall redistribute internal workload among its processors.
The Backend System shall be capable of accepting an aggregate data input stream of a minimum of 1.6 Gbytes/sec.
The Backend System shall be capable of delivering an output data stream of a minimum of 25 MBytes/sec.
The Backend System shall maintain input data fidelity and dynamic range across all processing functions.
The Backend System shall be self-monitoring to detect processor hardware failures and operating system hangs.
The Backend System shall continue to operate during the temporary loss of the e2e System.
The Backend System shall allow all software processes to be killable restartable and debuggable.
All users of the Backend System shall login using a unique identification.
The Backend System shall log all attempts to access the system.
The Backend System shall provide an administrator with unrestricted access to all aspects of the system.
The Backend System shall employ modular design principles to the maximum extent possible.
The original raw uncorrected data must be recoverable from the processing output.
